data.title: Hershey Unveils One Hershey Strategy at 2026 Investor Day
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The Hershey Company ( (HSY) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On March 31, 2026, The Hershey Company hosted its 2026 Investor Day at the New York Stock Exchange, where executives detailed a strategy to “Lead Next Generation Snacking” through an integrated “One Hershey” commercial model across sweet, salty and functional snacks. Management emphasized a five-year innovation pipeline focused on premium and better-for-you offerings, plans for a more automated and AI-enabled supply chain to boost productivity, and a clear roadmap to restore margins and deliver balanced top- and bottom-line growth.

The company reaffirmed its 2026 full-year guidance, projecting net sales growth of 4% to 5%, organic net sales growth of 2.5% to 3.5% and a sharp rebound in profitability, with reported EPS expected to rise 79% to 89% and adjusted EPS 30% to 35%, partly aided by its 2025 acquisition of LesserEvil. The outlook and strategic agenda underscore Hershey’s intent to sustain top-tier shareholder returns while solidifying its competitive position in the evolving global snacking landscape.

The most recent analyst rating on (HSY) stock is a Hold with a $200.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on The Hershey Company stock, see the HSY Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on HSY Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, HSY is a Neutral.

HSY scores positively on strong technical momentum and resilient cash generation, but the overall rating is held back by a sharp 2025 margin/earnings reset and a high P/E (~51) that increases downside if recovery is slower than guidance. The earnings call supports the outlook with clear recovery targets for 2026, though cocoa and pricing elasticity remain key risks.

To see Spark’s full report on HSY stock, click here.

More about The Hershey Company

The Hershey Company, listed on the NYSE under ticker HSY, is a global confectionery and snacking manufacturer best known for its iconic chocolate and candy brands. The company is increasingly positioning itself as a diversified snacking player, expanding from traditional sweets into salty, premium, better-for-you and functional snack categories to capture growth across the broader snacking market.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

data.title: Canagold Resources Files 2025 Annual Financials and Control Certifications with U.S. SEC
data.description:

An announcement from Canagold Resources ( (TSE:CCM) ) is now available.

On March 31, 2026, Canagold Resources Ltd. filed a Form 6-K with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission for the month of March 2026, furnishing its annual disclosure package for the year ended December 31, 2025. The submission included management’s discussion and analysis, consolidated financial statements, and the CEO and CFO certifications of those annual filings.

Chief executive officer Catalin Kilofliski certified that the 2025 annual filings were free of material misstatements and fairly presented the company’s financial condition, performance and cash flows. He also confirmed that Canagold’s disclosure controls and internal control over financial reporting were designed and evaluated using the COSO framework, with no reported material weaknesses in design, underscoring the company’s focus on governance and reporting reliability for investors.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:CCM) stock is a Buy with a C$1.20 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Canagold Resources stock, see the TSE:CCM Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CCM Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CCM is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial performance (no revenue, ongoing losses, and negative free cash flow), despite a low-debt balance sheet. Technicals add modest support due to a strong trend above moving averages, but overbought signals increase pullback risk. Valuation is also constrained by negative earnings and no provided dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on CCM stock, click here.

Canagold Resources Ltd. is a Canadian mining company based in Vancouver, British Columbia. It operates as a foreign private issuer in the U.S. capital markets, filing its disclosures under Form 20-F, which positions the company within the cross-border junior and mid-tier resource sector seeking access to both Canadian and U.S. investors.

data.title: Masonglory Limited Files September 2025 Interim Results, Showing Stronger Balance Sheet
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Masonglory Limited ( (MSGY) ).

On March 31, 2026, Masonglory Limited filed a Form 6-K with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, furnishing unaudited interim condensed consolidated financial statements as of September 30, 2025 and for the six months ended September 30, 2025 and 2024. The submission also included management’s discussion and analysis and full Inline XBRL data, signaling a step-up in financial transparency and regulatory compliance for shareholders and prospective investors.

The interim figures show that between March 31 and September 30, 2025, total assets rose to $11.65 million from $6.55 million, driven by higher contract assets, prepayments and new machinery, while total liabilities edged down to $2.95 million. Shareholders’ equity more than doubled to $8.70 million, supported by a sharp increase in additional paid-in capital and modestly higher retained earnings, even as revenue and net income for the six-month period declined year-on-year, suggesting capital-structure strengthening despite softer profitability.

More about Masonglory Limited

Masonglory Limited is a Hong Kong-based foreign private issuer listed in the United States, reporting under Form 20-F. The company operates through various subsidiaries and prepares its financial statements in U.S. dollars, indicating an international investor focus, although its specific operating industry and primary products or services are not detailed in this filing.

data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Huazhong In-Vehicle Holdings Company Limited ( (HK:6830) ) has issued an announcement.

Huazhong In-Vehicle Holdings Company Limited has proposed a final ordinary cash dividend of RMB 0.002479 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, equivalent to HKD 0.002826 per share based on an exchange rate of RMB 1 to HKD 1.14. The dividend is subject to shareholder approval on 3 June 2026, with the shares trading ex-dividend on 8 June, a book closure from 10 to 12 June, a record date of 12 June, and payment scheduled for 10 July 2026.

The announcement sets out a clear timeline for investors to establish dividend entitlement, reinforcing the company’s practice of returning cash to shareholders while providing transparency on key dates. The board composition, listing details and absence of withholding tax or warrant-related adjustments suggest a straightforward distribution structure for existing shareholders in the Hong Kong market.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:6830) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.23 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Huazhong In-Vehicle Holdings Company Limited stock, see the HK:6830 Stock Forecast page.

data.title: Fusen Pharmaceutical Narrows Annual Loss Despite Revenue Decline
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Fusen Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd. ( (HK:1652) ).

Fusen Pharmaceutical reported revenue of RMB273.4 million for 2025, down 16.2% year on year, with gross profit falling 32.5% and gross margin narrowing from 49.9% to 40.2%. The board decided not to distribute a final dividend for 2025, in line with the prior year.

Despite weaker top-line performance, the company sharply reduced its net loss attributable to shareholders to RMB11.8 million from RMB188.8 million, helped by lower selling, administrative and research expenses and improved contributions from a joint venture. Net finance costs increased slightly, but operating losses narrowed significantly, signaling some stabilization after a challenging 2024.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1652) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.72 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Fusen Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd. stock, see the HK:1652 Stock Forecast page.

More about Fusen Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd.

Fusen Pharmaceutical Company Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated drug maker listed in Hong Kong. The group operates in the pharmaceutical industry, generating revenue from the sale of medicines and related healthcare products in mainland China, with a focus on prescription and over-the-counter formulations.

Sino-Synergy Hydrogen Energy Technology (Jiaxing) Co., Ltd., a hydrogen energy technology company based in China, operates in the clean energy sector with a focus on hydrogen-related solutions. The company targets the growing hydrogen and fuel cell market, seeking to expand within China’s broader new energy and decarbonisation drive.

Xiamen Jihong Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2603) ) just unveiled an update.

Xiamen Jihong Co., Ltd. announced the current composition of its board of directors, led by chairwoman Zhuang Hao, and including executive directors, an employee representative director, and four independent non-executive directors. The disclosure also details the membership and chairmanship of four key board committees—strategy, audit, remuneration and appraisal, and nomination—highlighting the company’s governance framework and allocation of oversight responsibilities among directors.

By formalizing and publishing the roles and functions of each director across these committees, the company provides greater transparency to investors and other stakeholders about its decision-making and supervisory structure. This structure, with independent directors chairing critical committees such as audit, remuneration, and nomination, is designed to strengthen internal control, enhance oversight of management, and align with corporate governance best practices expected of Hong Kong–listed firms.

More about Xiamen Jihong Co., Ltd. Class H

Xiamen Jihong Co., Ltd. is a joint stock company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China and listed in Hong Kong under stock code 2603. The company is governed by a board comprising executive, independent non-executive, and employee representative directors, reflecting a typical listed-company governance structure aimed at balancing management and shareholder interests.

data.title: Ascletis Deepens R&D Push in Obesity Drugs as 2025 Loss Widens
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Ascletis Pharma, Inc. ( (HK:1672) ).

Ascletis Pharma reported a modest 5.2% increase in total income to RMB127.4 million for 2025 but saw its net loss widen by 19.6% to RMB359.9 million, driven mainly by a 35.3% surge in research and development spending to RMB409.1 million, while administrative and other expenses declined. The company highlighted substantial progress across its metabolic and immune disease pipelines, including multiple obesity-focused GLP-1, amylin receptor, and multi-agonist candidates advancing toward U.S. IND filings in 2026, positioning Ascletis to participate more aggressively in the fast-growing global obesity and diabetes drug markets despite near-term financial losses.

Ascletis’ lead program ASC30, an oral once-daily GLP-1 tablet, showed encouraging placebo-adjusted weight loss and favorable gastrointestinal tolerability in obesity, with Phase III trials slated for the third quarter of 2026 and an ongoing U.S. Phase II study in diabetes targeting topline data in the same period. The company also advanced a broad next-generation obesity portfolio, including long-acting subcutaneous depot formulations of ASC30, a THRβ-based muscle-preserving weight loss agent ASC47, several amylin receptor and GLP-1R/GIPR dual and triple agonist candidates (ASC35, ASC36, ASC37) and their fixed-dose combinations, with multiple IND submissions to the FDA planned between the second and third quarters of 2026, signaling an aggressive push to build a differentiated, multi-modal obesity franchise.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1672) stock is a Hold with a HK$13.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Ascletis Pharma, Inc. stock, see the HK:1672 Stock Forecast page.

More about Ascletis Pharma, Inc.

Ascletis Pharma Inc. is a Cayman Islands-incorporated biopharmaceutical company focused on developing innovative treatments for metabolic and immune diseases, with a growing emphasis on obesity and related metabolic disorders. Its pipeline includes oral and injectable small-molecule and peptide drug candidates targeting GLP-1, amylin receptors, THRβ, and multi-agonist pathways, with a clear strategy to compete in the global obesity and diabetes therapeutics market.

Average Trading Volume: 2,773,954

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$17.86B

Ruifeng Power Group Company Limited has declared a final ordinary cash dividend of HKD 0.03 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, payable in Hong Kong dollars. Shareholder approval is scheduled for 17 June 2026, with the ex-dividend date set for 24 June, a record date of 30 June, and payment due on 4 September 2026, with no withholding tax applicable.

data.title: Hengxin Technology Swings to 2025 Net Loss as Revenue Falls and Taxes Surge
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Hengxin Technology Ltd. ( (HK:1085) ) just unveiled an update.

Hengxin Technology Ltd. reported a 10.8% drop in revenue to RMB2.25 billion for 2025, with gross profit edging down 1.0% to RMB462.7 million but gross margin improving 2.1 percentage points to 20.6%. Despite tighter cost of sales and lower selling expenses, higher impairment charges, increased interest expense and a sharp rise in income tax turned a prior-year profit into a net loss attributable to shareholders of RMB42.4 million.

The group’s basic earnings swung from a profit of RMB0.097 per share in 2024 to a loss of the same amount in 2025, and the board decided not to recommend a final dividend for the year. The move to preserve cash, combined with weaker top-line performance, underscores mounting operational and financing pressures that may weigh on shareholder returns and could challenge the company’s competitive position in a slowing telecoms equipment market.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1085) stock is a Hold with a HK$1.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Hengxin Technology Ltd. stock, see the HK:1085 Stock Forecast page.

More about Hengxin Technology Ltd.

Hengxin Technology Ltd. is a mainland China-based manufacturer in the telecommunications infrastructure sector, producing cables and related connectivity products. The group focuses on supplying network operators and equipment makers, positioning itself within the broader communications and industrial cabling markets.
